title: Aspects: Reclaimed
description: An Aspects Addon introducing 7 new Aspects inspired by concepts that go beyond the base modification
body:
![Banner](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/Y3QzViMQ/images/a26b427556f27a70a28432935267a05fabf99caf.png) > This addon contains content that did not make it to the main modification. > Currently it contains 7 new Aspects with potentially more to come! ## Features ### Aspects - **Dragonkin:** Colossal and powerful winged dragon creatures unnaturally bound to a humanoid body. - **Frostling:** Dancer of Ice and very vocal haters of the hot. - **Pebblemite:** Born from the stone, seeks the stone, their bug like form makes them hard to catch, but also becomes their biggest weakness. - **Remnant:** Tall and capable of controling gravity itself. Can create teleportation points. - **Voidborne:** Void thing capable of storing mobs themselves and retrieving them on command. - **Phoenix:** Winged daredevils that seek nothing but the thrill of adrenaline. Great at lower health levels. - **Morphling:** Evolving and adaptable form changeling able to adapt to almost any environment.
See Available Aspects (check out Gallery for even more details) | Aspect | Effect(s) | Focus Ability(s) | |:----------|:-----------|:----------------| | **Dragonkin** | - Extremely tall with higher reach and health
- Much higher health and armor
- Starts with an Elytra that can hover
- More damage with a Dragon Head | - Grants Regeneration and Resistance | | **Frostling** | - Less health
- Stronger in Cold Biomes
- Weaker in Hot Biomes
- Faster on Ice | - Makes nearby ice denser | | **Pebblemite** | - Smaller and weaker
- Faster and stronger on stone-like blocks | - Teleports a very short distance through stone-like blocks | | **Remnant** | - Taller with more health
- Takes damage from water
- Has much less gravity pull
- Can increase gravity pull when sneaking
- Taking damage has a chance to randomly teleport the user | - Save a teleport position
- Teleport to saved location while saving current location | | **Voidborne** | - Smaller and weaker
- Sneaking teleports nearby items
- Taking damage has a small chance to randomly teleport the attacker | - Store a non-powerful entity
- Select and check stored entities
- Summon a selected stored entity
- Summon all stored entites at once | | **Phoenix** | - Starts with an Elytra that is accelerates faster
- Takes water damage
- Weaker when healthy
- Stronger the weaker it gets | - Damage self for Resistance
- Launch into the air while in Lava | | **Morphling** | - Smaller with Less Health
- Has unlockable forms Fire and Void
- Faster but taller in Fire form
- Tankier but even more taller in Void form
- Takes water damage in Fire and Void forms | - Forcefully change forms
- Dash in water in Aquatic form
- Damage self for an explosion centered on them
- Use Food Levels to Dash in Void form |
## In-Game Documentation and Configuration This extends the base mods documentation Access from the Pause Menu → **Data Packs...** → **Aspects** Includes: - List of Aspects and their information ## FAQ > *Why is there a missing Resource Pack?* Likely due to the downloaded Data Pack version. You **need** to download the bundled Resource Pack from the same download location where you got the Data Pack. The download for it can be found always on the version page directly, not on the main page of this modification. Below is an example what the Data Pack (top) and Resource Pack (bottom) downloads look like on Modrinth: ![Data Pack and Resource Pack download examples](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/Y3QzViMQ/images/d6fecb01cd0e74d4bea75dcfc462d500d42cf068.png) > *My Mods button is overlapping the Aspects button in the Pause Menu!* This issue can be the fault of the Mod Menu (Fabric) mod itself. Make sure that in the Mod Menu settings the **Game Menu** option is **NOT** set to **insert**, if it is, set it to anything else.
